channel.title: Deep Dive
channel.description: Personal deep-dive episodes on technology, science and how things work — researched and produced on demand.
channel.itunes:author: Sebastian Brenke
channel.itunes:summary: Personal deep-dive episodes on technology, science and how things work — researched and produced on demand.

item.title: Does Acupuncture Work - And How Would We Even Know?
item.description:
Millions swear by acupuncture and acupressure — but how do you scientifically test whether any alternative therapy actually works? Using acupuncture as the case study, we take the claims seriously, then walk through the traps that fool us — regression to the mean, the placebo effect, sham needles — and land on what the evidence honestly shows.

Sources

  • Acupuncture for Chronic Pain: Update of an Individual Patient Data Meta-Analysis (Vickers et al., Journal of Pain 2018)
  • Acupuncture for preventing migraine attacks (Cochrane review)
  • PC6 (P6) acupoint stimulation for postoperative nausea and vomiting (Cochrane review)
  • Is the Placebo Powerless? An Analysis of Clinical Trials (Hróbjartsson & Gøtzsche, NEJM 2001)
  • Placebo effects are weak: regression to the mean is the main reason ineffective treatments appear to work (DC's Improbable Science)
  • Acupuncture: a 'needle in the haystack' or a 'theatrical placebo'? (Edzard Ernst)
item.itunes:summary:
Millions swear by acupuncture and acupressure — but how do you scientifically test whether any alternative therapy actually works? Using acupuncture as the case study, we take the claims seriously, then walk through the traps that fool us — regression to the mean, the placebo effect, sham needles — and land on what the evidence honestly shows.

Sources

  • Acupuncture for Chronic Pain: Update of an Individual Patient Data Meta-Analysis (Vickers et al., Journal of Pain 2018)
  • Acupuncture for preventing migraine attacks (Cochrane review)
  • PC6 (P6) acupoint stimulation for postoperative nausea and vomiting (Cochrane review)
  • Is the Placebo Powerless? An Analysis of Clinical Trials (Hróbjartsson & Gøtzsche, NEJM 2001)
  • Placebo effects are weak: regression to the mean is the main reason ineffective treatments appear to work (DC's Improbable Science)
  • Acupuncture: a 'needle in the haystack' or a 'theatrical placebo'? (Edzard Ernst)

item.title: How Data Actually Travels - Down a Wire, Through Glass, and Across Thin Air
item.description:
Everything digital is just ones and zeros — but a copper wire, a strand of glass, and the open air don't know what a number is. So how does a bit physically travel? This episode follows a single one and zero through all three media, and finds the one idea they secretly share.

Sources

  • Line Coding — Network Encyclopedia
  • Digital-to-Digital Conversion — GeeksforGeeks
  • How fiber optics work: total internal reflection — AFL
  • Fibre Optic vs Copper — FiberMall
  • Sending data over RF signals — NetworkAcademy.IO
  • Understanding Modern Digital Modulation Techniques — Electronic Design
item.itunes:summary:
Everything digital is just ones and zeros — but a copper wire, a strand of glass, and the open air don't know what a number is. So how does a bit physically travel? This episode follows a single one and zero through all three media, and finds the one idea they secretly share.

Sources

  • Line Coding — Network Encyclopedia
  • Digital-to-Digital Conversion — GeeksforGeeks
  • How fiber optics work: total internal reflection — AFL
  • Fibre Optic vs Copper — FiberMall
  • Sending data over RF signals — NetworkAcademy.IO
  • Understanding Modern Digital Modulation Techniques — Electronic Design

item.title: How an RSS Feed Actually Works
item.description:
We just built a private podcast feed for this show — so what is RSS, really? A short explainer on the quietly resilient format behind every podcast: why a feed is just a text file, how the audio lives elsewhere, and why your app pulls new episodes rather than being pushed them.

Sources

  • RSS 2.0 Specification (Harvard Berkman Center)
  • Apple Podcasts feed requirements
  • WebSub — the W3C standard for push updates to feeds
item.itunes:summary:
We just built a private podcast feed for this show — so what is RSS, really? A short explainer on the quietly resilient format behind every podcast: why a feed is just a text file, how the audio lives elsewhere, and why your app pulls new episodes rather than being pushed them.

Sources

  • RSS 2.0 Specification (Harvard Berkman Center)
  • Apple Podcasts feed requirements
  • WebSub — the W3C standard for push updates to feeds

item.title: How RAM Is Made - And Why It Suddenly Costs So Much
item.description:
Why has RAM roughly doubled in price? This episode unpacks how DRAM is actually manufactured — the 1T1C cell, the brutal capacitor-etching that defines memory fabrication — and contrasts it with logic chips at a foundry like TSMC. Then it follows the money into HBM and the AI build-out to explain the 2026 memory shortage.

Sources

  • Samsung warns of memory shortages driving industry-wide price surge in 2026 (Network World)
  • Samsung and SK hynix warn AI-driven memory shortages could last until 2027 (Tom's Hardware)
  • DRAM and NAND prices jump as Samsung, SK Hynix and Micron tighten supply (Astute Group)
  • DRAM: Device Fabrication (Entegris)
  • Why DRAM is stuck in a 10nm trap (Blocks & Files)
  • Scaling the Memory Wall: The Rise and Roadmap of HBM (SemiAnalysis)
  • Why is HBM so Hard to Manufacture? (Vikram Sekar)
  • SK hynix Partners With TSMC to Strengthen HBM Leadership
item.itunes:summary:
Why has RAM roughly doubled in price? This episode unpacks how DRAM is actually manufactured — the 1T1C cell, the brutal capacitor-etching that defines memory fabrication — and contrasts it with logic chips at a foundry like TSMC. Then it follows the money into HBM and the AI build-out to explain the 2026 memory shortage.

Sources

  • Samsung warns of memory shortages driving industry-wide price surge in 2026 (Network World)
  • Samsung and SK hynix warn AI-driven memory shortages could last until 2027 (Tom's Hardware)
  • DRAM and NAND prices jump as Samsung, SK Hynix and Micron tighten supply (Astute Group)
  • DRAM: Device Fabrication (Entegris)
